sanskrit:
यस्त्विन्द्रियाणि मनसा नियम्यारभतेऽर्जुन।
कर्मेन्द्रियैः कर्मयोगमसक्तः स विशिष्यते॥
transliteration:
yas tv indriyāṇi manasā niyamyārabhate ’rjuna |
karmendriyaiḥ karma-yogam asaktaḥ sa viśiṣyate ||
literal_meaning: One who, having restrained the senses by the mind, O Arjuna, begins karma-yoga with the organs of action, unattached—he excels.
contextual_meaning: True psychological maturity is not sensory suppression but mindful regulation: enlist the body’s ‘doing’ capacities in purposeful work while the observing mind remains non-clinging.
psychological_state: Integrated will—senses are neither indulged nor denied; they are instruments steered by an unattached mind toward meaningful action.
trigger_scenario: A professional caught between binge-scrolling escapism and rigid self-denial learns to set app timers, then channels the same phone into creating helpful content without obsessing over likes.
anchor_text: Use your senses skillfully while keeping your mind free from clinging.
